Course Title: AI in Financial Decisions: When Accuracy Isn’t Enough
Category: Finance & Professional Services: Banking, Insurance, Accounting, Consulting, Legal, Real Estate
Who is this for: Financial advisors, loan officers, underwriters, accountants, insurance agents, risk analysts, compliance officers, branch managers, portfolio managers, credit analysts
Team or Dept this is for: Finance, Risk Management, Compliance, Banking Operations, Insurance, Accounting, Investment Services, Credit, Audit
Description: Why “mostly correct” can still create major financial risk.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with everyday financial concepts such as budgeting, lending, or insurance is helpful, along with a general curiosity about how AI tools are beginning to influence financial decision-making.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key limitations of AI-generated outputs in financial contexts and explain why a high accuracy rate does not guarantee reliable decision-making. You will also apply practical frameworks to evaluate AI recommendations critically, helping you protect clients, manage risk, and uphold professional standards across banking, insurance, accounting, and related fields.
